title: Indian Foreign Secretary assures continued support for Sri Lanka
body_lines: Foreign Secretary of India Shri Vinay Kwatra has assured the fullest support of the Indian government for Sri Lanka to ride out the ongoing crisis situation.
He made this remark when four high-ranking Indian officials called on Sri Lankan President Gotabaya Rajapaksa earlier today at the President’s House in Colombo.
The four-member delegation arrived in Sri Lanka this morning for a one-day visit to hold bilateral talks on the assistance provided to Sri Lanka by India amidst the ongoing economic crisis.

Led by the Indian Foreign Secretary, the delegation comprises Secretary of the Department of Economic Affairs of the Ministry of Finance of India Ajay Seth, Chief Economic Advisor to the Government of India Dr. V. Anantha Nageswaran and Joint Secretary of the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) Kartik Pande of the Ministry of External Affairs of India.

Upon reviewing the shipments of fuel, medicine, fertilizer and essential food items Sri Lanka has already received under loan assistance from India, the delegation reiterated the commitment of the Indian government and political authorities to provide continued support to Sri Lanka in the future as well.
In response, President Rajapaksa expressed gratitude to the Indian government and the people of India for the support extended to Sri Lanka as the island nation grapples with a dire economic crisis.
Further, a lengthy discussion was held between the two sides on stabilizing the economy of Sri Lanka and the future course of action of the Indian loan assistance.
